reasoning: The base rate of NATO invoking Article 5 is extremely low, having only occurred once in the alliance's 75-year history (following the September 11 attacks in 2001). The time period in question is roughly 7 months (late May to December 2025). While geopolitical tensions are currently elevated due to the war in Ukraine and instability in the Middle East, the threshold for invoking Article 5 is exceptionally high. Accidental cross-border strikes (such as the 2022 missile incident in Poland) have been handled diplomatically without invoking Article 5 to avoid direct escalation. Deliberate, large-scale armed attacks on NATO territory by state actors like Russia remain highly unlikely to occur in this short timeframe, as deterring such an attack is the core purpose of NATO. Given the short exposure window and overwhelming reluctance of major powers to risk a full-scale direct conflict, the likelihood remains close to the historical base rate.
